reasoning: The flagged script (install-desktop-icon.sh) poses no risk during makepkg execution as it's not invoked by PKGBUILD functions. Qwen's 'unsafe' verdict stems from misapplying runtime concerns to build-time security context.
disagreement_analysis: Qwen incorrectly classified install-desktop-icon.sh as unsafe due to path injection risks during user runtime, while Deepseek correctly recognized it's irrelevant to makepkg execution.
coverage_issues: Both models missed reviewing .install hooks (if present) and deeper analysis of wrapper script execution context
Qwen reviewed only 6 files vs Deepseek's 12, skipping critical build artifacts like qt.conf
false_positives: Qwen's 'unsafe' verdict on install-desktop-icon.sh (runtime issue, not build-time threat)
Qwen's concern about dlmain.c path handling (normal upstream code)
false_negatives: Both missed potential analysis of library removal in PKGBUILD (though appears safe)
No model reviewed post-install hook behaviors or desktop file execution context
learnings: Improve scope awareness: Train models to strictly differentiate build-time (makepkg) vs runtime risks
Enhance file prioritization: PKGBUILD hooks > install scripts > binaries > configs > static assets
Add validation for path safety assessments: Distinguish between build-time paths vs user-controlled runtime paths
Implement cross-report consistency checks to flag scope misinterpretations
